mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing the llm-d deployment.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64

chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64

sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Download the latest CLI binary for Linux.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in a chosen region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are running and ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Install llm-d
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart to deploy the inference serving stack.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in its own namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/examples/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the llm-d Helm release.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your configurations and data. This step involves upgrading the llm-d deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n llm-d -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Drain all pods in the llm-d namespace.
> Upgrade the llm-d Helm release.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n llm-d -o name | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application is crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust configurations or resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or resize existing nodes in the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes API server is not reachable, possibly due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network configuration and ensure the cluster is running.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in a 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
